content:
# Thixotropy

**Summary**: The property of a fluid to become thicker or more viscous over time when left at rest.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2012-amer|Amer, 2012]] analyzed thixotropy to determine how quickly the slurry would develop high gel content.

- [[2014-elhag|ELHAG, 2014]] assessed the thixotropic behavior of drilling fluid.

- [[2022-adjei|Adjei, 2022]] evaluated the thixotropic behavior of the cement slurries using gel strength and hysteresis loops.

- [[2023-al-dogail|AL-DOGAIL, 2023]] evaluated thixotropic behavior to understand the time-dependent changes in viscosity during pumping.

- [[2024-mahmoud|Mahmoud, 2024]] evaluated the thixotropic behavior of the drilling fluid to ensure it could recover its structure after being subjected to shear.

## Scientific Context

- [[2012-amer|Amer, 2012]] critical for determining the pumpability and stability of the cement during placement.

- [[2014-elhag|ELHAG, 2014]] assessed the thixotropic behavior of drilling fluid.

- [[2022-adjei|Adjei, 2022]] evaluated the thixotropic behavior of the cement slurries using gel strength and hysteresis loops.

- [[2023-al-dogail|AL-DOGAIL, 2023]] evaluated thixotropic behavior to understand the time-dependent changes in viscosity during pumping.

- [[2024-mahmoud|Mahmoud, 2024]] evaluated the thixotropic behavior of the drilling fluid to ensure it could recover its structure after being subjected to shear.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2012-amer|Amer, 2012]] monitored via consistency (Bc) over time.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Higher concentrations of Nano silica led to higher thixotropy, meaning the slurry developed high gel faster during static periods. ([[2012-amer|Amer, 2012, p. 90]])


- The upward and downward speed schedule is to assess the thixotropic behavior of drilling fluid. ([[2014-elhag|ELHAG, 2014, p. 60]])

- The LWS slurry exhibited intrinsic thixotropic behavior without the addition of specific thixotropic agents, likely due to GGBFS content. ([[2022-adjei|Adjei, 2022, p. 80]])

- The viscosity of the EAS decreased with time under constant shear rates, indicating thixotropic behavior. ([[2023-al-dogail|AL-DOGAIL, 2023, p. 148]])

- Claytone-IMG 400 enhanced the thixotropic behavior, allowing it to thin under shear stress and recover its initial viscosity once the stress was removed. ([[2024-mahmoud|Mahmoud, 2024, p. 78]])
